component.name: Container
component.description: sbomify's production container image. VEX justifications for High advisories embedded in the osv-scanner and cosign CLI binaries that are bundled to run the OSV and signature-verification plugins. These are operator tooling invoked as subprocesses on trusted inputs, not sbomify runtime code.

vulnerabilities.description: Moby (github.com/docker/docker) AuthZ plugin bypass with an oversized request body, embedded in the osv-scanner binary's Docker SDK.

analysis.detail: github.com/docker/docker is vendored into the osv-scanner binary for its container-image scanning mode. sbomify invokes osv-scanner only against lockfile-derived SBOM/source inputs supplied by the platform; it never points osv-scanner at a Docker daemon, so the Moby AuthZ-plugin code path is never executed. sbomify will pick up the fix when a future osv-scanner release bumps its vendored Moby.

vulnerabilities.description: Moby (github.com/docker/docker) vulnerability embedded in the osv-scanner binary's Docker SDK.

analysis.detail: Vendored into osv-scanner for its Docker container-scanning mode, which sbomify never invokes (osv-scanner is run against SBOM/lockfile inputs, never a Docker daemon). Fixed when osv-scanner bumps its vendored Moby.

vulnerabilities.description: Docker Engine (github.com/docker/docker) vulnerability embedded in the osv-scanner binary's Docker SDK.

analysis.detail: Vendored into osv-scanner for its Docker container-scanning mode, which sbomify never invokes (osv-scanner is run against SBOM/lockfile inputs, never a Docker daemon). Fixed when osv-scanner bumps its vendored Moby.

vulnerabilities.description: Go standard library net/textproto resource exhaustion (DoS) when decoding a maliciously-crafted MIME header; the cosign v3.1.1 binary is built with Go 1.26.3 (fixed in Go 1.26.4).

analysis.detail: Exploitation requires parsing MIME headers from an attacker-controlled HTTP peer. sbomify's cosign binary is invoked by the signature-verification plugin and contacts only trusted sigstore infrastructure (Rekor, Fulcio) and the configured OCI registry, so the malicious-peer precondition is not present. cosign v3.1.1 is the latest release; sbomify will rebuild on the next cosign release compiled with Go 1.26.4+.
